first_steps.title: First steps
first_steps.sub: Everything you need to know in your first month. No fluff.
first_steps.sin_title: How to get your SIN Number
first_steps.sin_lead: The Social Insurance Number (SIN) is your tax identifier in Canada. Without it you cannot work legally or access government benefits.
first_steps.sin_step1: You need your valid passport and your work or study permit (or your PR card if you are already a permanent resident).
first_steps.sin_step2: Find the nearest office at Canada.ca/service-canada. No appointment needed in most cities.
first_steps.sin_step3: The process is free and you receive your number the same day on a printed slip. Keep it in a safe place.
first_steps.sin_step4: Share it only with your employer, bank and the CRA. Never send it via unsecured email.
first_steps.sin_tip: If you arrived on a tourist visa and don't yet have a work permit, you must wait until you have one before applying for a SIN.
first_steps.banca_lead: With your passport and proof of address (or an employer letter) you can open an account in your first days. Most banks have special programs for newcomers.
first_steps.banca_tip: You need your SIN before your bank appointment. Open the account within the first 15 days to simplify your first payroll deposit.
first_steps.banca_rbc_pros: Branches across Canada, solid app, good Spanish support in major cities.
first_steps.banca_rbc_cons: Monthly fees if you don't meet the minimum balance.
first_steps.banca_td_pros: Extended hours including weekends. Good options for newcomers.
first_steps.banca_td_cons: The newcomer account has a limited validity period.
first_steps.banca_scotiabank_pros: StartRight program for immigrants. Credit card with no credit history required.
first_steps.arriendo_title: Renting: avoid scams and find your place
first_steps.arriendo_lead: The rental market in {{country}} is competitive. Knowing the rules and warning signs saves you money and legal headaches.
first_steps.arriendo_scam1: Landlord "out of the country" asking for a deposit before showing you the property
first_steps.arriendo_scam2: Price well below the local market
first_steps.arriendo_scam3: Payment requested in cash or transfer without a receipt
first_steps.arriendo_scam4: No formal lease or pressure to sign quickly
first_steps.arriendo_scam5: You cannot visit the property in person before paying
first_steps.arriendo_tip: In most provinces, the landlord can ask for a maximum of 1 month deposit. Always demand a receipt and a written lease before handing over any money.
first_steps.trabajo_lead: Before job hunting, make sure your SIN is active and understand what type of work permit you have (open or closed).
first_steps.trabajo_step1_title: Adapt your CV to the Canadian format
first_steps.trabajo_step1: No photo, no age, no marital status. 1-2 pages max. Include only relevant experience and quantifiable skills.
first_steps.trabajo_step2: LinkedIn (essential), Indeed.ca, Workopolis and provincial government job portals.
first_steps.trabajo_step3: Attend chamber of commerce events, industry groups on Meetup.com and LinkedIn communities in your city.
first_steps.trabajo_step4: If it's your first local experience, look for volunteering or entry-level work to build your reference network in {{country}}.
first_steps.trabajo_tip: Many provinces have programs to recognize foreign credentials. Check your province's portal to validate your professional degree.
first_steps.visas_lead: Recognized Organizations (RO) are entities authorized by IRCC to offer Working Holiday spots within the IEC (International Experience {{country}}) program.
first_steps.visas_step1: They are private or non-profit organizations approved by the Canadian government to process IEC applications. They act as intermediaries between the applicant and IRCC.
first_steps.visas_step2: Direct IRCC spots fill up fast. ROs have reserved spots and can submit your application even when the direct pool is closed.
first_steps.visas_step3: ROs charge a service fee in addition to IRCC fees. The process includes registration, document review, invitation to apply (ITA) and permit application.
first_steps.visas_step4: Check the updated list on Canada.ca by searching "IEC Recognized Organizations". Always verify the organization appears on that official list before paying any fee.
first_steps.visas_tip: ROs do not guarantee the visa, they only facilitate the process. The final outcome depends on IRCC. Be wary of anyone promising guaranteed approval.
first_steps.visas_cta_text: Looking for professional help with your immigration process?

stats.ticker_3: 🐾 Your review helps the next migrant

community.title: Real stories from the Manada in Canada
community.sub: Not statistics. People just like you who already took the leap.
community.cristobal_quote: I found work in demolition in less than two weeks. The hardest part was finding housing and managing payments when I arrived. La Manada helped me with all the document information before leaving Chile.
community.valeria_quote: I arrived from El Bosque with very basic English and a lot of fear. La Manada told me to just go for it — and they were right. You learn it much faster here than in any course.
community.nandy_quote: I found childcare work before leaving Chile. I arrived with everything organized thanks to La Manada: travel insurance, orientation, logistics. French was the hardest part, but a bilingual family helped me adapt fast.

value_props.title: The information you need,
value_props.lead: Verified directory, active community, and a WhatsApp group where over 500 Spanish-speaking migrants already support each other.
value_props.item1_title: By the community, for the community
value_props.item1_body: Every provider was recommended or validated by real Manada members. We don't list strangers.
value_props.item2_body: One click on WhatsApp and you're talking directly to someone who can help. No forms, no waiting.
value_props.item3_body: Directory, first steps guides, remittance comparator and active community. One place for your arrival in Canada.

how_it_works.title: How does it work?
how_it_works.lead: From "I don't know who to call" to talking with someone you trust in three steps.
how_it_works.step1_body: Insurance, immigration, work, housing, languages. Everything organized, no noise.
how_it_works.step2_body: Name, service, countries, languages, and whether they're verified by the Manada community.
how_it_works.step3_body: One click opens WhatsApp. No forms. No waiting. Talk to them today.

trust_badge.title: What does
trust_badge.lead: It's not a legal certificate. It's something more valuable: validation from real people who have already worked with this provider and recommend them.
trust_badge.disclaimer: ⚠️ SoyManada is a reference directory. It does not provide legal, immigration, or financial services. Always evaluate your provider before hiring.
trust_badge.pillar1_body: Every provider was suggested or endorsed by real Manada members. We don't list strangers.
trust_badge.pillar2_body: We validate real presence: active profile, community history, and direct contact method.
trust_badge.pillar3_body: Being on SoyManada can't be bought. It's earned through trust. The badge is merit-based, not money-based.

registro.campo_paises: In which country does your service primarily operate?

registro.terminos: I confirm that the information provided is accurate and authorize SoyManada to publish the business name, description, and contact channels in the directory if my application is approved. I understand that SoyManada reserves the right to accept or reject applications.
registro.error_generico: Something went wrong. Please try again.
registro.exito_mensaje: We'll review your information and contact you at the provided email if your profile fits the directory. Thank you for wanting to be part of SoyManada.

registro_proveedores.title: Your next client
registro_proveedores.subtitle: SoyManada connects trusted providers with thousands of migrants who need real help. No expensive ads, no algorithms. Just direct trust.
registro_proveedores.cta_primary: I want to appear on SoyManada
registro_proveedores.cta_note: We review all applications · Response within 48 hours
registro_proveedores.benefit1_body: Thousands of migrants search for trusted services every week. Your profile will be right where they look.
registro_proveedores.benefit2_body: Users contact you via WhatsApp or Instagram. No middlemen, no commissions.
registro_proveedores.benefit3_body: If you meet the criteria, you receive the "Verified by Manada" badge. Worth more than any ad.
registro_proveedores.benefit4_body: Appearing on SoyManada has no cost. Community trust is the only requirement.
registro_proveedores.step1_body: Complete the form with your name, service, countries, and contact info.
registro_proveedores.step2_body: Our team validates your information and community presence.
registro_proveedores.step3_body: Your profile goes live and you start receiving direct contacts.
registro_proveedores.final_cta_body: It's free, it's simple, and it reaches people who need what you offer.

pricing_page.title: Choose how you
pricing_page.subtitle: From basic visibility to priority positioning. No commitment. Cancel anytime.
pricing_page.bronze_desc: To start being visible in the community
pricing_page.silver_desc: For providers who want to grow on the platform
pricing_page.gold_desc: Maximum visibility and tools to scale
pricing_page.feature_verified_criteria: Verified badge (if you meet the criteria)
pricing_page.note: Prices in USD · Chile: Bronze free · Silver $9,500 CLP/mo · Gold $19,000 CLP/mo · No commitment
pricing_page.faq1_q: Can I change plans at any time?
pricing_page.faq1_a: Yes. You can upgrade or downgrade whenever you want. Changes take effect in the next billing cycle.
pricing_page.faq2_q: How does the verified badge work on Bronze?
pricing_page.faq2_a: On Bronze, the badge is granted manually if you meet the community criteria. On Silver and Gold, the verification process is prioritized and guaranteed.
pricing_page.faq3_q: Do you accept payments from Chile?
pricing_page.faq3_a: Yes. We accept international cards and local transfers for both Canada and Chile.
pricing_page.faq4_a: No. All plans are month-to-month. Cancel anytime from your profile, no questions asked.
pricing_page.bottom_body: Thousands of Spanish-speaking migrants search for trusted services every week.

founder.quote: Migrating alone is hard. Doing it with the right pack changes everything.
founder.bio: I'm a migrant. I know what it costs to arrive in a new country without knowing anyone — not knowing who to trust with your visa, your money, or your mental health. SoyManada was born from that frustration, so the next migrant doesn't have to search alone.
founder.name: Francisco Aleuy
